messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command do?

The static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command creates a static binding that associates an IP address in the current pool with a specific MAC address. This causes the DHCPv4 server to only assign the specified IP address to a client station with the specified MAC address. The no form of this command removes the specified binding. <IPv4-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. The IP address must be within the address range defined for the current pool. Specifies a client station MAC address (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Public static-bind 185 hostname <HOST> Specifies the host name of the client station. Range: 1 to 255 pr intable ASCII characters. Syntax: static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dhcpv6-server leases command do?

The clear dhcpv6-server leases command clears DHCPv6 server lease information. The DHCPv6 server must be disabled before clearing lease information. Clears leases for all VRFs. Clears the lease for a specific client on a specific VRF. Specify t he client address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Public clear dhcpv6-server leases 188 <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zeros (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hextet of four zeros to a single 0. For example, this address 2222: 0000: 3 333: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 3333: : 4444: 55. vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears leases for a specific VRF. Syntax: clear dhcpv6-server leases [all-vrfs | <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME>] | vrf.

The static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>] command creates a static binding that associates an IP address in the current pool with a client identifier or DUID. This causes the DHCPv6 server to only assign the specified IP address to a client station with the specified client identifier or DUID. The no form of this command removes the specified static binding from the pool. Public static-bind 203 <IPv6-ADDR> Specifies the IP address to assign in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xx xx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal num ber from 0 to F. For example, this address 2222: 0000: 333 3: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 3333: : 4444: 55. client-id <ID> Specifies the client identifier or DUID. hostname <HOST> Specifies the host name of the client station. Range: 1 to 255 pr intable ASCII characters. Syntax: static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>].

section: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/IP Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? ?

4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? : The factory default VLAN to which the DHCP address is assigned is VLAN1. Users can configure the VLAN for DHCP allocation on platforms supporting multiple VLAN configurations. Only a single VLAN can support DHCP assignments. 5. Can DHCP assignments be disabled in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch? Use the no ip dhcp command on the required VLAN interface or management VRF port you can disable the DHCP assignments in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch. A static configuration option is available on both ports. 6. Can IP DHCP switch assignment be carried out on a Routed only Port (RoP)? No, RoP is not supported. Only VLAN and OOBM ports can be allocated an address with DHCP. 7. Can an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch support DHCP IPv6 Allocation? Yes, an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch can support DHCP IPv6 allocation. However, for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP), IPv4 is required. 8.

topic: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the switch flash storage to populate its local cache. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping trust command do?

The dhcp-snooping trust command enables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Only server packets received on trusted ports are forwarded. All the ports are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables DHCPv6 snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCPv6 snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCPv6 snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcpv6-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IPv6 bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained after the switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IPv6 bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command do?

The preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command enables a DHCPv6 snooping guard policy to allow or deny the DHCPv6 servers in the specified server preference range. If not configured the minimum preference is set to 0 and maximum preference is set to 255. The no form of the command removes the server preference limits on the specified DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. minimum <VALUE> maximum <VALUE>. Syntax: preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE>.

section: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/IP Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN?

5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN: a. Check if the server is reachable on the same VLAN as the client. b. If the server is on a different L3 network, configure DHCP-Relay to forward the packets between the client and server. 6. Authorized server configurations-When configured, the server assigning IP address should be one of the IP addresses from the authorized server list. In DHCP snooping IPv6, if DHCP Relay is between a test device and a server, the authorised server configuration should include a link to the local IP address of the relay interface. This is because, while forwarding the server's response to the client, it will stamp a link to the local IP address as the source IP of the packet.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about IP-Binding mismatch in VSX topologies?

IP-Binding mismatch in VSX topologies: 1. Both primary and secondary VSX peers should have the same clock value. If not, there is a possibility of inconsistent ip-bindings that could result in ip-binding sync in a loop. There is no straight-forward way to check the inconsistencies but by looking at the number of bindings and debug log analysis 2. Ensure the ISL state is up and operational. DHCP client IP traffic is dropped at data path 1. This can happen when IP-Source-Lockdown is used along with DHCP snooping. Ensure IP-Bindings are learned for clients that are experiencing traffic failure issues 2. Check the hardware programming state of the IP-Binding entry by IP-Source-Lockdown. Use show i pv4 source-lockdown bindings or show IPv6 source-lockdown bindings commands. Public.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[:PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command the http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command specifies HTTP proxy location and VRF. When HTTP proxy location and VRF are configured on the switch, it overrides any existing HTTP proxy location and VRF as this has the highest priority over the values obtained from other sources. Following locations can be used for the HTTP proxy location: • A fully qualified domain name (FQDN). • An IPv4 address with colon separated port number Public DHCP Options commands 270 • An IPv6 address with colon separated port number NOTE When configuring an IPv6 address with a port number, the address must be specified inside square brackets. An example - [2001: 0db8: 85a3: 0000: 0000: 8a2e: 0370: 7334]: 8080. If the command is entered without the VRF parameter, then the VRF used will be 'default' VRF. Syntax: http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[:PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of ND bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max bindings applies. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. <MAX-BINDINGS> Specifies the maximum number of ND bindings. You can use the show capacities command to see the maximum available for yo ur switch model. Syntax: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping ra-drop command do?

The nd-snooping ra-drop command this command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) drop on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress RA packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on both trusted and untrusted ports. When disabled, RA packets are forwarded on the selected VLAN with ND snooping trusted port validation. RA drop is disabled by default. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the config context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. The no form of the command disables ND snooping RA drop on the selected VLAN. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-drop.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the DNS client?

To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns. Commands shown in the procedure include: VRF Name: default.

document_title: Ip Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch Series)
section: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/IP Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A?

Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A: L3-GW(config)# vlan 100 L3-GW(config-vlan-100)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface 1/1/25 L3-GW(config-if)# vlan trunk allowed 100 L3-GW(config-if)# no shutdown L3-GW(config-if)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip address 192.168.1.1/24 Now the clients will be able to ping the gateway IP; but will not be able to communicate with each other due to the isolation enforced with private VLAN. As expected, the ping from client 1 to client 2 will fail in the above example, although the clients are in the same subnet.

topic: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

document_title: Ip Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch Series)
section: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/IP Services Guide (5420, 6200 Switch series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A?

Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A: If local proxy ARP is enabled in the L3 gateway, the L3 gateway responds to ARP requests for addresses in the same subnet. In other words, the L3 gateway will be doing proxy for ARP resolution. L3-GW(config)# no ip icmp redirect L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip local-proxy-arp With this, the L3 communication will be enabled between client 1 and client 2. Now the ping from client 1 to client 2 will be successful in the above example. The ARP table in client 1 will be populated with the MAC of L3 gateway; not the MAC of client 2. Similar ARP table entry for client 2 as well. In other words, the L3 gateway is doing proxy for ARP resolution for the local network where clients are isolated at layer 2. Dynamic ARP inspection Dynamic arp inspection is provided as a mechanism for making ARP more secure.

topic: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command do?

The arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv4 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Specifies the IP address of the neighbor or the virtual IP addres s of the cluster in IP format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255.. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor or the multicast M AC address in IANA format (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a h exadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Defaul t: 131072. Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command do?

The debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command enables ARP security debug logs. If <SEVERITY> is omitted, all severities are logged. The no form of this command disables ARP security debug logs. <LOG-CATEGORY> Selects the ARP security debug log category. Available categori es are: • all: Selects all ARP security debug log categories. • config: Selects the ARP security config debug log category. inspection: Selects the ARP security inspection debug log • category. Public debug arp-security 353 severity <LEVEL> • packet: Selects the ARP security packet debug log categor y. Specifies how to filter the ARP security debug logging by settin g the minimum severity level for which debug logging will be pe rformed. The selected severity level and all severities above (m ore severe) will be included in the logging. Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip local-proxy-arp command do?

The ip local-proxy-arp command enables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Local proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces and on VLAN interfaces. To enable local proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no ip local-proxy-arp command disables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Public ip local-proxy-arp 355 uplink-passthrough (For 10000 Switch series only) Enables local proxy ARP passth rough to uplink members. NOTE When enabling ip local-proxy-arp uplink- passthrough, ip local-proxy-arp will be imp licitly enabled. Syntax: ip local-proxy-arp.

The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. <IPv6-ADDR>> mac <MAC-ADDR>> Specifies an IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for Cache Configuration?

Cache Configuration: Inactive Timeout: 120 Active Timeout: 1800 Role-based IPFIX If IPFIX monitoring is configured on a port, a network administrator who wants to monitor traffic from clients must preconfigure IPFIX monitor before onboarding the clients. Also, if a client moves from one port to another, the monitor must be reconfigured accordingly. To reduce all these complexities, 5420, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switch series allow you to configure an IPFIX monitor on a port access role. An IPFIX deployment with monitoring configured on a port uses TCAM to get flow statistics. Since TCAM is a high-demand resource which is shared across multiple protocols including the security protocols, security policy applications could fail if IPFIX consumes most of the TCAM resources. Port access clients can use a Public Role-based IPFIX 28 large amount of of TCAM resources for policy applications, making it difficult support port access clients with TCAM based IPFIX. With role-based IPFIX, when a user plugs a device into to a specific port, their user role is applied to the port.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow record <record-name> command do?

The flow record <record-name> command creates or modifies a flow record and switches to the config-flow-record context for the flow record. Define data to be included in a flow record by configuring flow record match and collect fields. A flow record defines match (key) fields and collection (non-key) fields. Customers configure flow records with match (key) fields and collect (non-key) fields. Match fields are the set of fields that define a flow, such as IP address or UDP port. Collect fields are the set of fields that identify information to collect for a flow, such as packet and byte counters. Traffic with matching attributes (for example, traffic coming from the same interface, sent to the same destination with the same protocol) are classified as a single flow. Syntax: flow record <record-name>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow-tracking command do?

The flow-tracking command configuresFlow tracking for TCP and UDP flows, and optionally, ICMP flows. The no form of this command deletes the flow tracking configuration context. In order to optimize the flow removal process, flows that have aged-out are flushed in batches. A flow that has aged out is flushed only when the next batch processes. This can cause some flows to stay inactive for a slightly longer time than the value configured here. EnablesFlow tracking. Configures an age-out time for ICMP flows, in seconds. Range: 10 to 86400. Default: 15. Configures global concurrent flow limit for flow tracking enable d interfaces. Range: For 6200 Switch series, 64 to 5000. For 54 20 Switch series, 64 to 10000. Default: none. flow ConfiguresFlow parameters. statistics ConfiguresFlow statistics. Syntax: flow-tracking.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command do?

The ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command enable flow monitoring on inbound and outbound interfaces by assigning a flow monitor to that interface. Only physical interfaces and LAG interfaces can be monitored. A flow monitor cannot be applied to an interface that is part of a LAG. If an unsupported application is attempted, an error message will be displayed. If the flow monitor is associated with a flow record that contains application fields as collect fields, then Application Recognition should be enabled on the same interface. The [no] form of command disables the flow monitoring. Syntax: [no] ip|IPv6 flow monitor (interface).

The show flow exporter command displaysFlow exporter configuration and status. When no exporter name is specified, the output of this command displays information for all flow exporters. The output of this command can indicate the following status types: • Accepted • Rejected (Internal error: exporter does not exist) • Rejected (Internal error: destination type does not exist) • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but no destination is specified) Public show flow exporter 58 • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but the specified hostname or IP address is invalid) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but no destination is specified) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but the specified Traffic Insight instance does not exist) • Rejected (Destination type is Tr. Syntax: show flow exporter [<name>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply congestion-event profile command do?

The apply congestion-event profile command applies a congestion event profile to an interface. The no form removes the congestion event profile from the interface. Specifies the name of a congestion event profile. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), un derscore (_), and hyphen (-). In order for the congestion event profile entry associated with an applied congestion event profile to be effective, the interface needs to be configured for queue-monitor. Once the queue statistics are being monitored, apply the congestion event profile to the interface to begin the process of actively checking the monitored queue statistics for congestion events based on the thresholds configured in the applied congestion event profile. Syntax: apply congestion-event profile [<NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-sla responder command do?

The ip-sla responder command selects the IP-SLA responder. The responder can be configured for udp-echo, tcp-connect, udp-jitter-voip type. It requires the SLA name, SLA type, and port number as arguments. The no form of this command removes the IP-SLA responder. Specifies the IP-SLA responder name. Length: 1 to 64 chara cters. <SLA-NAME> udp-echo tcp-connect Enables responder for udp-echo probes. Selects TCP connect as the IP-SLA test mechanism. udp-jitter-voip Selects VOIP jitter as the IP-SLA test mechanism. Specifies the port number to listen for IP-SLA probes. Range: 1 to 65535. Public ip-sla responder 127 source {<SOURCE-IPv4- ADDR>|<SOURCE-IPv6-ADDR>| <IFNAME>} Selects the source IPv4 or IPv6 address for SLA probes or the s ource interface to use for sending IP-SLA probes. Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> (udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip).

The VLAN command specifies the port identifier range. Shows brief info in tabular format. Shows the physical connection info in tabular format. Shows additional statistics, including the tx filtered and rx filte red counters. • Rx filter packets are protocol packets received when the p rotocol is disabled on the switch and there is only one port i n the VLAN. Protocols include OSPF, PIM, RIP, LACP, and LL DP. • An example of a Tx filtered packet would be a multicast pac ket being filtered from going out of the ingress port. Shows statistics rounded to the nearest power of 1000, for exa mple, 1K, 345M, 2G. This is available only in the CLI interface o utput. Shows only non zero statistics. Shows LAG interface information. Continuously monitor interface statistics. Shows loopback interface information.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the destination cpu command do?

The destination cpu command the command causes the mirror session to transmit mirrored packets to the switch CPU. This destination may be configured for multiple sessions, however only one such configured session may be active at a given time. The diagnostic utility Tshark may be used to view and capture packets transmitted to the CPU through this route. Ctrl+C must be entered to terminate a Tshark capture session. More details can be found in the Supportability Guide. The no form of this command will immediately stops mirroring traffic to the CPU, but will not remove any sources from the mirror configuration. Syntax: destination cpu.

Creating a mirror session and adding a VLAN as a source of traffic in both directions on that port: switch# configure terminal switch(config)# mirror session 1 switch(config-mirror-1)# source vlan 10 both Creating a mirror session and adding two VLANs as sources of traffic: directions: Public source vlan 189 switch# configure terminal switch(config)# mirror session 2 switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 10 tx switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 20 both Configuring the source in session 2 to receive by specifying the source interface configuration: switch(config-mirror-2)# source vlan 10 rx Removing the first source interface in session 2 entirely, and removing the transmit direction from.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command do?

The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Specifies the name of the context. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASC II characters, excluding space and question mark (?). Public snmpv3 context 256 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF associated with the context. Default: default. community <STRING> Specifies the SNMP community string associated with the con text. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question mark. Default: public. Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id in colon separated hexadecimal n otation. <ENGINE-ID>. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command do?

The snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. • no snmpv3 security-level auth-privacy: Sets the security level to no authentication or privacy, allowing any SNMP user to connect. SNMP v3 users that support authentication, or authentication a nd privacy are allowed. Only SNMP v3 users with both authentication and privacy are al lowed. This is the highest level of SNMP v3 security. Default. Syntax: snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy}.

The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Specifies the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent wil l use. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 161. <PORT>. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting Public snmp-server community 262 to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 4100i, 6000, and 6100 only support default VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Specifies the name of a VRF. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the queue-monitor command do?

The queue-monitor command this command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to collect queue statistics. then display this information as a queue statistics history. These aggregate statistics can be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and Public Regulatory Information 299 performance anomalies. This feature is disabled by default, but can be enabled on a maximum of 52 interfaces per switch. Syntax: queue-monitor.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure QoS?

To configure QoS, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure how local priority values are assigned to ingress packets with the commands qos cos-ma 2. Optionally, add a rate limit for ingress traffic on one or more interfaces with the command rate-lim. Commands shown in the procedure include: QoS configuration 27.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure threshold profiles?

To configure threshold profiles, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a threshold profile defining one or more queue threshold configurations with a WRED action 2. Apply the threshold profile globally (all ports), or apply the threshold profile as a port override to any.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear queue-monitor interface command do?

The clear queue-monitor interface command clear the data collected on an interface since enabling queue monitoring. If no interfaces are specified, perform a clear of data collected on all interfaces with queue monitoring enabled. Name of the interface. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> <IFRANGE> Range of interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT>-<MEMBER>/<SLOT> /<PORT> Public clear queue-monitor interface 42. Syntax: clear queue-monitor interface [<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dwrr queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> [weight <WEIGHT>] command do?

The dwrr queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> [weight <WEIGHT>] command assigns the deficit weighted round robin (DWRR) algorithm and its weight to a queue in a schedule profile. DWRR allocates available bandwidth among all non-empty queues in relation to the queue weights. Use show qos schedule-profile <NAME> to view the settings of a specific schedule profile. The no form of this command removes the DWRR algorithm from a queue in a schedule profile. Public dwrr queue 44 Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. Specifies the scheduling weight. Range: 1 to 1023. Syntax: dwrr queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> [weight <WEIGHT>].

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Quality of Service Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER> command do?

The map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER> command assigns a local priority to a queue in a queue profile. By default, the larger the queue number the higher its priority. A queue without a local priority value assigned to it is not used to store packets. The same queue can be assigned multiple local priorities. The no form of this command removes the specified local priority from a specific queue. If no local priority number is specified, then all local priorities are removed from the queue. Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. <QUEUE-NUMBER> <PRIORITY-NUMBER>. Syntax: map queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> local-priority <PRIORITY-NUMBER>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> command do?

The name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER> command assigns a description to a queue in a queue profile. This is for identification purposes and has no effect on configuration. The no form of this command removes the description associated with a queue. Specifies the queue number. Range: 0 to 7. <QUEUE-NUMBER> <DESCRIPTION> Specifies a queue description for identification purposes. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), undersco re (_), and hyphen (-). Examples Assigning the description priority-traffic to queue 7: switch(config)# qos queue-profile myprofile switch(config-queue)# name queue 7 priority-traffic Removing the description from queue 7: switch(config)# qos queue-profile myprofile switch(config-queue)# no name queue 7. Syntax: name queue <QUEUE-NUMBER>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos cos command do?

The qos cos command configures a CoS PCP remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. Packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured CoS PCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If QoS trust mode is not set to none, then the remark is ignored, and the following commands will show the CoS remark status as ignored (incompatible Port Access Trust configuration) or not applied' (incompatible QoS global/port Trust configuration): • show running-configuration • show interface <PORT-NUM> • show interface <PORT-NUM> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Specifies an 802.1 VLAN priority CoS value. Range: 0 to 7. <CODE-POINT>. Syntax: qos cos <CODE-POINT>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. Public qos queue-profile 59 A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory-default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}} command do?

The rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}} command sets the amount of traffic of a specific type that can ingress on an Ethernet interface, or on each port of a LAG interface. Rate limits are enforced separately on each individual member of a LAG, not on the LAG as a whole. The no form of this command removes the traffic limit for the specified traffic type. {broadcast|multicast| unknown-unicast|icmp {ip- all|ip|IPv6} <RATE> {kbps|percent|pps} Specifies the type of ingress traffic to which the rate limit appli es: broadcast, multicast, unknown-unicast, or ICMP. The multicast rate limit affects multicast and broadcast traffic. The broadcast rate limit only affects broadcast traffic. When bot h types are applied to the same interface, broadcast packets a re limited to the lower of the two rate values. Syntax: rate-limit {broadcast|multicast|unknown-unicast|icmp {ip-all|ip|IPv6}}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface queue-monitor command do?

The show interface queue-monitor command display queue statistics history in the CLI as either a list or a histogram, or as a table, showing the previous five minutes of queue statistics history. Public show interface queue-monitor 83 interface [<IFNAME>] Specifies the name of an Ethernet port or LAG on the switch. If the optional <IFNAME> parameter is omitted, the output of this command displays all queue statistic data for all interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> or lag <ID> histogram|list|table Specifies the output format for queue monitor output. • histogram - Queue statistics history is presented in histog • ram format for the last 8 hours. list - Queue statistics history is presented in the list form at for the last 8 hours • table - Queue statistics history is presented in tabular for mat for the last 5 minutes. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>} queue-monitor.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki application command do?

The crypto pki application command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

document_title: Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

Syntax: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki certificate command do?

The crypto pki certificate command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Public crypto pki certificate 635 Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

document_title: Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki est-profile command do?

The crypto pki est-profile command creates a certificate Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST) profile and changes to the config-est-<EST- NAME> context for the profile. Each EST profile stores information about the EST service, including EST server URL Up to 16 profiles can be created. If the specified EST profile exists, this command changes to the config-est-<EST-NAME> context for the profile. Public crypto pki est-profile 674 The no form of this command deletes the specified EST profile. It also deletes the TA profiles whose CA certificates were downloaded from the corresponding EST server, and the leaf certificates that were enrolled using this EST profile. NOTE The deletion of the related TA profiles and enrolled certificates is permanent. Syntax: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>.

Syntax: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show crypto pki est-profile command do?

The show crypto pki est-profile command shows a list of all configured EST profiles, or detailed information for a specific profile. Specifies the EST profile name. Range: Up to 32 alphanumeric c haracters (excluding "). <EST-NAME> Within the EST profile context, configures the URL of the certificate enrollment EST server. This is not configured by default. Any existing URL is replaced by this command. The no form of this command removes the EST server URL within the selected EST profile. The removal of the URL does not affect the TA profiles and enrolled certificates from the EST server. Specifies the EST server URL. Range: Up to 192 characters. <URL>. Syntax: show crypto pki est-profile [<EST-NAME>].

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply macsec policy command do?

The apply macsec policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MAC sec policy to the selected port. When a MAC sec policy is applied to a port, MAC sec is enabled on the port and all data traffic is blocked on the port until a secure channel is successfully established. NOTE A MAC sec policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. If a MAC sec policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. NOTE For MAC sec to work, an MKA policy must also be configured and applied to the same ports. The no form of this command dissociates the specified policy from the port. Syntax: apply macsec policy <MACSEC-POLICY-NAME>.

document_title: Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the apply macsec policy command?
Syntax: apply macsec policy <MACSEC-POLICY-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear macsec statistics command do?

The clear macsec statistics command clears MAC sec statistics on all MAC sec-enabled interfaces or on a specific interface or interface range. MAC sec statistics are cleared for the entire switch rather than just in the current user session. interface <IF-RANGE> Specifies one or more interfaces for which MAC sec statistics in formation is to be cleared. Syntax: clear macsec statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>].

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply mka policy command do?

The apply mka policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MKA policy to the selected port. To start the MKA protocol on the port, a MAC sec policy must also be applied to the port. NOTE An MKA policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. If an MKA policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. The no form of this command dissociates the specified policy from the port. Specifies the MKA policy name. Range: 1 to 32 alphanumeric ch aracters including only the three special characters "." (period), " -" (hyphen), and "_" (underscore). <MKA-POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: apply mka policy <MKA-POLICY-NAME>.

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

Syntax: apply mka policy <MKA-POLICY-NAME>

document_title: Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series)
source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear mka statistics command do?

The clear mka statistics command clears MKA statistics on all MAC sec-enabled interfaces or on a specific interface or interface range. MKA statistics are cleared for the entire switch rather than just in the current user session. interface <IF-RANGE> Specifies one or more interfaces (ports) for which MKA statisti cs information is to be cleared. Configures the MAC sec policy to use data delay protection. Data delay protection allows MKA participants to ensure that the data frames protected by MAC sec are not delayed by more than 2 seconds. Enabling data delay protection necessitates transmission of MKPDUs at a frequency of 0.5 second to meet a maximum data delay of 2 seconds while minimizing connectivity interruption due to the possibility of lost or delayed MKPDUs. Syntax: clear mka statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-metho... 827 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h a u t h - m e t h o. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

source_file: 5420/10_17_1000/Security Guide (5420, 6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series).md

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-rea... 828 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h c a c h e d - r e a... The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius ser... 833 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h r a d i u s s e r... When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command do?

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command configures the data client limit on the multidomain enabled interface. By default, the data client limit on a multidomain enabled interface is 1, and the maximum number of data clients supported on a multidomain enabled port is 5. Specifies the maximum data client limit on the multidomain ena bled interface. Range: 1 to 5. <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show aaa authentication port-access interface command do?

The show aaa authentication port-access interface command shows information about the status of the role applied on ports. RADIUS overridden user roles are suffixed with *. The role name is not displayed for clients that do not use local, downloaded, or RADIUS overridden role. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Public show aaa authentication port-access interface clie... 878 s h o w a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s i n t e r f a c e c l i e... Specifies the client MAC address. <MAC-ADDRESS>. Syntax: show aaa authentication port-access interface {all | <IFRANGE>}.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access clients command do?

The show port-access clients command shows summarized active port access client information. NOTE The User-Role column in the output will not display any value for clients not using local, downloaded or RADIUS overridden role. When an explicit client name is not available, only the MAC address of the client will be displayed. The VLANs in the output display the tags, u, t, and multi to indicate untagged VLAN, single tagged VLAN, and multiple VLANs respectively. Shows DHCP information of port access clients. dhcp NOTE To view the DHCP information of port access cl ients, either client IP tracker or DHCP snooping must be enabled. If client IP tracker is enabled, then the command does not display the lease ti me. This command does not display information about tagged VLAN. Shows port access information about UBT clients. Syntax: show port-access clients [dhcp-info|ubt|vxlan] [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access port-security violation clie command do?

The show port-access port-security violation clie command shows information on the number of client-limit-exceeded security violations that have occurred. The output can be filtered by interface. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name or a comma-separated list of inte rfaces, or a hyphen-separated interface range. Syntax: show port-access port-security violation client-limit-exceeded interface.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x supplica command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x supplica command enters the 802.1X supplicant global configuration context. Enters the 802.1X supplicant port context. NOTE The 802.1X supplicant is only supported on L2 physical interfaces that are not members of a LAG. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x supplicant.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command do?

The clear dot1x supplicant statistics command clears the 802.1X supplicant statistics associated with the interface. If no interface is specified, the statistics are cleared for all 802.1X supplicant-enabled interfaces. Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant statistics are cleared. <IFRANGE> Configures the time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator on the other end before considering the link to be non-802.1X-capable and opening the interface on the data-plane. On a timeout, the switch will not use the authentication result to determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap. If not set, the switch will wait for the 802.1X authentication cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the interface. Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa radius-attribute group command do?

The aaa radius-attribute group command configures an existing RADIUS server group for which the configured RADIUS attributes will be included in request packets. Enters the config-radius-attr context. Public Configurable RADIUS attribute commands 1055 The no form of this command unconfigures the RADIUS server group for the configured RADIUS attributes. NOTE Nas-id and tunnel-private-group-id attributes only apply to port access requests. Nas-ip-addr attributes only apply to management user requests. Specifies an existing RADIUS server group name. <GROUP-NAME> For the selected (by context) RADIUS server group, configures the Network Access Server (NAS) ID request type for which the attribute configured with command nas-id value will be included. The no form of this command unconfigures the specified request type. Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear radius-proxy profile statistics command do?

The clear radius-proxy profile statistics command clears the RADIUS proxy profile statistics. The statistics are reset on the system, not just the current session. Selects the name of the RADIUS proxy profile. Maximum of 32 c haracters. <PROFILE-NAME> Configures the RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy client for the client group. A client group can have a maximum of 16 dynamic authorization clients. The no form of this command removes the client from the client group. Specifies the client IP address or hostname. <HOSTNAME|IPv4|IPv6> vrf<VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: clear radius-proxy profile statistics <PROFILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip (RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy profil command do?

The ip (RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy profil command configures RADIUS proxy IP address for the profile. This is a mandatory parameter for the functioning of the proxy feature. The no form of this command removes the RADIUS proxy IP address from the profile. RADIUS proxy IP address. <IPv4|IPv6>. Syntax: ip <IPv4|IPv6>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip (RADIUS proxy profile) command do?

The ip (RADIUS proxy profile) command configures the RADIUS proxy IP address for the profile. This is a required parameter for the functionality of the proxy feature. The no form of this removes the RADIUS IP address from the profile. Public ip (RADIUS proxy profile) 1095 RADIUS proxy IP address. <IPv4|IPv6> Configures the Network Access Server identifier value. The NAS ID replaces the existing NAS ID value from the RADIUS packet while sending the packet to the home server. The no form of this removes the Network Access Server identifier value. ASCII string. Minimum length is 1 character and maximum lengt h is 253 characters. <WORD>. Syntax: ip <IPv4|IPv6>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-proxy enable command do?

The radius-proxy enable command enables RADIUS proxy at the global level. RADIUS proxy is disabled by default. This command is required for the RADIUS proxy functionality to work on the switch. The no form of this command disables the RADIUS proxy functionality. Syntax: radius-proxy enable.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-proxy profile command do?

The radius-proxy profile command configures the RADIUS proxy profile. The profile has all of the client and the server details to facilitate the proxy functionality. The profule must be enabled at the current context level after configuring all of the required parameters along with enabling the RADIUS proxy globally. A maximum of 32 RADIUS profiles can be configured on the device. The no form of this command removes the RADIUS proxy profile. Selects the name of the RADIUS proxy profile. Maximum of 32 c haracters. <PROFILE-NAME>. Syntax: radius-proxy profile <PROFILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show radius-proxy profile command do?

The show radius-proxy profile command displays the RADIUS proxy profile information. IP, client-group, server group, and enable must be configured for the profile. If these are not configured for the profile, the profile status is shown as config- incomplete. Specifies the name of the profile. Maximum of 32 characters. <PROFILE-NAME>. Syntax: show radius-proxy profile <PROFILE-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show running-config (radius-proxy) command do?

The show running-config (radius-proxy) command displays the current running configuration. Configures the RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. The proxy waits for this time interval to get a response from the home server. This value should be equal to or greater than the NAS RADIUS timeout value. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS server timeout interval to its default value of 5 seconds. RADIUS server timeout interval in seconds. Default: 5 seconds. <SECONDS>. Syntax: show running-config.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vrf (RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy profi command do?

The vrf (RADIUS dynamic authorization proxy profi command configures the VRF to use in the profile. The no form of this command restores theVRF to the default VRF in the profile. Specifies the VRF name. Default: default. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: vrf <VRF-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access port-security interface clie command do?

The show port-access port-security interface clie command shows port security clients status information for the ports. The output can be filtered by interface or MAC address. Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Specifies the client MAC address. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>}.

The show port-access port-security interface port command shows port security statistics for the ports in a switch. The output can be filtered by interface. Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the class gbp-ip command do?

The class gbp-ip command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv4 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv4 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Public class gbp-ip 1195 Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv4 packets. {match | ignore} <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> <MAX-PORT> <ICMP-TYPE-VALUE> <ICMP-CODE-VALUE> <app-category-name> Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Syntax: [no] class gbp-ip <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the class gbp-IPv6 command do?

The class gbp-IPv6 command creates, deletes, or modifies an IPv6 Group-Based Policy (GBP) class to match specified protocol packets.. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on IPv6 protocol header information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified IPv6 packets. Public class gbp-IPv6 1202 <SRC-ROLE-NAME> <DST-ROLE-NAME> <PORT-NUMBER> <MIN-PORT> <MAX-PORT> <ICMP-TYPE-VALUE> <ICMP-CODE-VALUE> <app-category-name> Specifies the source role name. Specifies the destination role name. Specifies the layer 4 port number. Range: 0 to 65535. Syntax: class gbp-IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the class gbp-mac command do?

The class gbp-mac command creates, deletes, or modifies class to match specified protocol packets. A class consists of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each class can classify traffic based on MAC information. The no keyword deletes either a class or an individual class entry. Specifies the class name. <CLASS-NAME> <SEQUENCE-NUMBER> {match | ignore} Specifies the class entry sequence number. Range: 1 to 429496 7295. Creates a rule to ignore or match specified packets. Specifies the source role name. Public class gbp-mac 1208 Specifies the destination role name. <DST-ROLE-NAME> <NUMERIC-ETHERTYPE> Specifies the Ether Type number. Range: 0x600 to 0xffff. Syntax: class gbp-mac <CLASS-NAME>.

messages.content: For 5420 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear port-access gbp hitcounts command do?

The clear port-access gbp hitcounts command clears the statistics of the group based policy applied on the client. Public clear port-access gbp hitcounts 1210 Specifies the CDP TLV number. Supported values are 1 to 6, 10, and 11. <TLV-NUMBER> Enables group based policy (GBP). The no form of this command disables group based policy (GBP). Maps the role name to the role ID. This mapping is used in the GBP encapsulation. For HPE Aruba Networking 6300 and 6400 Switch series, default roles internet and intranet are used to differentiate traffic from different fabrics or networks. It is necessary to ensure that the reserved system role names are available before upgrading to AOS-CX 10.13 or later. Syntax: clear port-access gbp [<POLICY-NAME>] hitcounts {client}.
